zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln於1206年出生在英國林可爾史爾林肯時，她的父親Robert de Quincy, the Younger27歲，母親Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln26歲。她於1221年6月21日之前與Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln在瑞士爾利易利瑙-福迪康費爾斯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1266年3月30日在英國英格蘭倫敦哈普斯亞德享年60歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦易斯林頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln于1206年出生在英国林可爾史爾林肯时，她的父亲Robert de Quincy, the Younger27岁，母亲Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln26岁。她于1221年6月21日之前与Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln在瑞士爾利易利瑙-福迪康費爾斯特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1266年3月30日在英国英格兰倫敦哈普斯亞德享年60岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦易斯林頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Quincy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in France deriving their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Quintus, meaning ‘fifth(-born)’ + the locative suffix -acum. The earliest bearer of the name in England, that of Saer de Quincy, ancestor of the earls of Winchester, was from Cuinchy in Pas-de-Calais, but the name may have been reinforced by immigrants from Quincy-sous-Sénard in Seine-et-Oise or Quincy-Voisins in Seine-et-Marne. A family of the name de Quincy held the title Earl of Winchester 1207–65. Descendants of this family later bore the name Quincey up to the time of the English essayist Thomas De Quincey (1785–1859), who changed his name from Quincey to De Quincey in 1796.

History: The American Quincy family were established in MA by Edmund Quincy in 1633. Fifth in descent was Josiah Quincy (1744–75), a leading patriot, who was sent to England to argue the colonists’ case in 1774. His son Josiah (1772–1864) was a powerful opponent of slavery, president of Harvard, and mayor of Boston, a post also held by several of his descendants. The traditional pronunciation is “Quinzy”.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln was born in 1206, in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Robert de Quincy, the Younger, was 27 and her mother, Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln, was 26. She married Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln before 21 June 1221, in First, Illnau-Effretikon, Zürich, Switzerland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 30 March 1266, in Hampstead, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Islington, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln est née en 1206, à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Quincy, the Younger, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln avant 21 juin 1221, à First, Illnau-Effretikon, Zurich, Suisse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 mars 1266, à Hampstead, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Islington, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln 1206 in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Quincy, the Younger 27 und ihre Mutter Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln 26. Sie hat vor 21. Juni 1221 in First, Illnau-Effretikon, Zürich, Schweiz Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. März 1266 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Hampstead, London, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Islington, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln è nata il nel 1206 nel Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Robert de Quincy, the Younger, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln prima del 21 giugno 1221, nel First, Illnau-Effretikon, Zurigo, Svizzera. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 30 marzo 1266, nel Hampstead, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolta a Islington, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincolnが1206年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアリンコルンで生まれた時，父Robert de Quincy, the Youngerは27歳，母Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincolnは26歳であった。1221年6月21日以前にスイスズuリチイルナウ-エフレティコンフィルストでSir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincolnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1266年3月30日にイギリスイギリスロンドンハンプステアドで60歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンイスリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln(이)가 1206년 영국 링코실르 링코컨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Quincy, the Younger(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1221년 6월 21일 이전 스위스 즈u리치 이나-프레티콘 피르스트에서 Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1266년 3월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 함프스테아드에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 이슬링턴에 묻혔다.

pt.from 21 July 1257 to 2 June 1258: de 21 de julho de 1257 a 2 de junho de 1258
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln nasceu em 1206, em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Quincy, the Younger, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln antes 21 de junho de 1221, em First, Illnau-Effretikon, Zürich, Suiça. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 30 de março de 1266, em Hampstead, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 60 anos, e foi sepultada em Islington, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Quincy , Countess of Lincoln родилась в 1206 г. в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Quincy, the Younger было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln перед 21 июнь 1221 г. в местности фирст, иллнау-еффретикон, зuрич, Швейцария. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 30 марта 1266 г. в местности хампстид, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности ислингтон, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
